name: Antibiotic stewardship: volume of antibiotic prescribing
title: Antibiotic stewardship: volume of antibiotic prescribing
description: Prescription items for all oral antibacterial drugs (excluding antituberculosis drugs and antileprotic drugs) per 1000 patients
why_it_matters: Antibiotics play a critical role in modern medicine, both in treating common infections and in preventing infections associated with procedures such as surgery and chemotherapy.

Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) occurs when microorganisms that cause disease are no longer affected by antimicrobial medicines. The development of resistance can be accelerated by factors such as the inappropriate use of antibiotics, making infections more difficult to treat and increasing the risk of complications.

The UK 5-year action plan for AMR includes ambitions to reduce unnecessary antibiotic use. This measure shows the volume of oral antibiotic prescribing relative to practice population size. Higher prescribing rates may indicate potential overuse and opportunities to improve antimicrobial stewardship.
numerator_bnf_codes_filter: ~0501130H0 #Exclude methenamine as this is a urinary antiseptic

change_log.description: Comprehensive update - rename measure, move to list size as denominator, restrict to only oral antibiotics, exclude antituberculosis and antileprotic drugs.
Exclude methenamine from the numerator as it is considered a urinary antiseptic not an antibiotic. Update text.
